lectureTitle: Guest: IAM Use Cases and Implementation Strategy

drills.question: An enterprise wants to govern who can touch which assets without ad-hoc per-user grants. What does IAM 'Management' actually entail here?
drills.modelAnswer: Management means overseeing and governing the access methods through which identities reach assets. It is done with IAM tooling and a centralized team that applies security best practices and compliance guidelines, rather than scattered manual grants. This gives consistent, auditable access control across the enterprise.
drills.citation: "overseeing and governing these access methods using IAM tools" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 03

drills.question: After a merger, access grants have multiplied uncontrollably across two combined orgs. What IAM governance moves rein this in?
keyBeats: Segregation of duties via org-based rights
drills.modelAnswer: Establish a centralized governance and recertification process so accesses are periodically reviewed and revoked, curbing the access proliferation a merger creates. Combine this with org-based rights and segregation of duties so an employee's relationship to the organization, not ad-hoc assignment, drives entitlements.
drills.citation: "Establish centralized governance & recertifiation to reduce access" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 06

drills.question: B2B partners need self-service and admin rights, but you cannot manage every partner user centrally. Which access-control model does the enterprise IAM use?
drills.modelAnswer: Use a policy-based authorization system (ABAC-style) with fine-grained API access control and central entitlement management, while delegating administration of partner users to the partner. This scales authorization decisions by policy/attribute rather than enumerating per-user grants centrally.

drills.question: An org wants assignments to follow job role/attributes so onboarding auto-grants the right access. What design step enables this in the reference architecture?
drills.modelAnswer: Do role/attribute mining to define roles, then drive provisioning from identity attributes (HR source). At Nuuday most entitlements are 'birthright' (auto-granted by role) or assigned via indirect business logic, with only a small fraction directly assigned — this is RBAC/ABAC in practice reducing manual grants.
drills.citation: "Entitlements assigned as part of birth right: 49.441" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 25

drills.question: Privileged service accounts and Tier 0 assets are the crown jewels an attacker wants. How does the enterprise IAM control privileged access?
keyBeats: PAM installed for critical assets + service accounts
Privileged Access Management as architecture pillar
drills.modelAnswer: Privileged Access Management (PAM) is deployed to handle critical assets and service accounts, with computer assets classified into tiers (Tier 0, Tier 1) so the most sensitive systems get the tightest controls. PAM is a distinct pillar of the IAM reference architecture for privileged/legacy access.
drills.citation: "PAM is installed and ready to handled crtical assets and service accounts" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 25

drills.question: Regulators and auditors demand evidence that access is appropriate and compliant. How does centralizing identity data help meet GDPR/HIPAA-type requirements?
keyBeats: Centralized identity info = single integration point
Provides data points for auditing & compliance
drills.modelAnswer: Centralizing all identity-related information gives a single point of integration and supplies the data points needed for auditing and compliance. It eases integration with SIEM systems to satisfy regulatory requirements like GDPR and HIPAA and to support threat, DDoS and ransomware detection.
drills.citation: "data points for Data-driven decision making, auditing & compliance" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 05

drills.question: Leadership won't fund a full IAM platform up front. How do you sequence access-control/governance delivery to still earn ROI?
keyBeats: Deliver value with small achievable goals
Scale platform maturity to company strategy
drills.modelAnswer: Plan an MVP and deliver value through small achievable goals to build sponsor confidence, realizing ROI incrementally. Use vendor SMEs/system integrators to lay the fundament, transfer knowledge to the internal team, then scale platform maturity in step with company strategy ('we build, we maintain').
drills.citation: "Deliver value with small achievabel goals" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 23

drills.question: Who decides IAM strategy, vendor choice and roadmap so access policy isn't set ad hoc by each team?
drills.modelAnswer: A centralized IAM governance body is set up to own IAM strategy and roadmap, run the vendor selection process, decide single-vs-multiple program structure, and anchor the program in the right IT department. This ensures access control and policy decisions are coordinated, not fragmented.
drills.citation: "Setup a centralized IAM Governance body responsible for IAM strategy" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 21

drills.question: A consumer must reach many digital properties without re-authenticating, yet access must stay scoped. Which access-management features serve this?
drills.modelAnswer: Provide Single Sign-On across digital properties for a uniform experience, backed by MFA/adaptive authentication and fine-grained access so each session is appropriately scoped. Delegated administration handles cases like family subscriptions where one account manages others.
drills.citation: "Single Sign-On across digital properties" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 17

drills.question: Two merged companies each ran their own employee lifecycle. What identity-lifecycle problem must IAM solve, and how?
drills.modelAnswer: Each company had its own employee lifecycle, so IAM must rationalize them into a single Joiner-Mover-Leaver process. Unifying JML standardizes how identities are created, changed on role moves, and deprovisioned on exit, preventing orphaned or duplicated accounts after the merger.

drills.question: Identity provisioning is only as good as its source data. Why is HRMS data quality central to the identity lifecycle?
keyBeats: Improve data quality in source systems (HRMS)
Enables segregation of duties + org-based rights
drills.modelAnswer: Provisioning and authorization derive from source identity data, so improving HRMS data quality is foundational. Clean source data lets IAM establish the correct employee-to-organization relationship, which in turn enables segregation of duties and organization-based rights across the lifecycle.
drills.citation: "Improving data quality in source systems like HRMS" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 05

drills.question: A company split means one enterprise account must become two. What lifecycle/identity work does separation force?
keyBeats: Migrate user privileges to new org structure
Carve out apps with their accesses
drills.modelAnswer: Separation requires splitting the single enterprise account, migrating user privileges to the new organization structure, and implementing the correct segregation of duties. All IT applications and their accesses must be carved out, effectively re-running identity lifecycle and provisioning for the new entity.
drills.citation: "Migration of user privileges according to the new organization structure" — IAM-Use-Case.txt, PAGE 07
